title: Air-fryer buffalo wings
subtitle: Crisp wings tossed in hot butter sauce
excerpt: Chicken wings dry-brined overnight, cooked at 200°C for 22 minutes, then tossed immediately in a sauce of Louisiana hot sauce and melted butter. The wings must go into the sauce hot: the butter coats the crisp skin and the sauce clings to every ridge. Serve with blue cheese dip.
sourceNotes: Buffalo wings were created at the Anchor Bar in Buffalo, New York in 1964. Teressa Bellissimo tossed leftover chicken wings in a hot sauce and butter mixture. The combination became the defining American bar snack. The air-fryer version applies the same sauce to wings cooked dry rather than deep-fried, and the result is close enough to the original that it has become the standard home method in Britain.

content.text: Never sauce the wings before cooking. The sugar in hot sauce burns under air-fryer heat, and the coating prevents the skin from crisping. The sauce goes on after the wings come out of the basket, hot from the fryer, and the residual heat absorbs the butter into the skin.

Pat the {{chicken-wings}} dry, toss with {{sea-salt-fine}} and {{baking-powder}}, and refrigerate uncovered on a wire rack for at least 8 hours. When ready to cook, preheat the air fryer to 200°C for 3 minutes. Arrange the wings in a single layer and cook at 200°C for 22 minutes, flipping halfway, until deeply golden and crisp. Meanwhile, whisk the {{louisiana-hot-sauce}}, {{unsalted-butter}}, and {{garlic-powder}} together in a large bowl. Pull the wings from the basket immediately and toss in the buffalo sauce while still hot. Serve at once with blue cheese dip and celery sticks.

items.fix: Whisk the butter and hot sauce together until they form a smooth, glossy emulsion rather than two separated liquids. The wings should go into the sauce while steaming hot
items.cause: Sauce not emulsified properly or wings not hot enough when sauced
items.symptom: Sauce too thin and sliding off the wings

items.fix: Reduce the hot sauce to 40 ml and increase the butter to 80 g. Taste the sauce before tossing the wings: adjust the ratio until the heat level is manageable
items.cause: Louisiana-style hot sauce varies enormously in heat level between brands

items.fix: Toss and serve immediately. Buffalo wings do not hold: they should go from basket to sauce to table in under 2 minutes
items.cause: Wings sat in the sauce too long
items.symptom: Sauce went on but wings softened

content.text: Buffalo wings are a Friday-night or game-night dish. The air fryer version removes the deep-fat fryer and produces something very close to the original. The sauce ratio here is classic: equal parts hot sauce and butter by weight. Serve with blue cheese or ranch dip, celery sticks, and cold beer.
